evidences.title: KT Rolster vs T1 Worlds 2025 grand final: Best odds, match ...
evidences.content: T1 is by far the favorite to win this grand final match, primarily because of the team’s proven track record. It won the last two World Championship events and has some of the biggest names in the League of Legends scene on its roster. There’s also the head-to-head record between the two teams, where T1 has 34 historical wins against KT’s 13. [...] The bottom lane will be much more interesting. T1’s Keria is an excellent and consistent support who has made game-winning plays for his team multiple times before. But KT’s deokdam has showcased more carry potential than T1’s Gumayusi, which may make the bottom lane a coin toss. The team that can get its ADC online first may be the one to win that particular game, as was seen in the third game between Gen.G and KT, where deokdam’s Ashe dominated the laning phase against Ruler’s Sivir and comfortably won his team the game. [...] ## KT Rolster vs T1 betting odds snapshot | Market | Odds | --- | | KT Rolster to win | 2.40 | | T1 to win | 1.58 | | Total maps under 4.5 | 1.50 | LoL bettingodds via Stake, 01:26 pm ET October 7, 2025. ## KT Rolster vs T1 match prediction Ultimately, everything here boils down to consistency, where KT has T1...

T1 LoL news T1 secured promotion to the semifinals of the League of Legends World Championship 2025 on Friday, with an impressive 3-2 victory against China’s Anyone’s Legend. T1 have always performed well against LoL Pro League (LPL) teams on the international stage. And Friday’s affair was not an anomaly in Lee “Faker” Sang-hyeok and his crew’s long history of beating Chinese teams. [...] The fifth and final map of the series will go down in history as one of the closest and most intense in LoL Worlds history. Anyone’s Legend started the decider slow and found themselves in a -1k gold deficit by the 12th minute. However, a series of solid skirmishes gave them a lead, which AL expanded to a +4k gold advantage by the 27th minute. But while Anyone’s Legend were accumulating gold lead, T1 focused on neutral objective control, securing all four drakes, while denying the Chinese squad an attempt on Baron. evidences.content: If KT Rolster win the final with a score of 3–0, they will repeat the legendary run of SK Telecom T1 (2015), when they lost only one map throughout the tournament. KT Rolster – LoL Team T1 – this is a story of stability under pressure. After a difficult group stage, the team gradually gained form and is now in “championship mode”. Faker seems not to age – he has not allowed any Chinese roster to upset him in Worlds playoffs. This is a team against which there is no right to make a mistake: one miscalculation – and the game is over. They are created to win. [...] In my opinion, the MVP of the series will be Keria – the success of T1 in the fight for key map objectives will depend on his ability to initiate teamfights. A finale-worthy clash of experience vs unyielding aggression. T1 ended groups 3-2, dropping 0-1 to CTBC Flying Oyster and Gen.G but sweeping FlyQuest (1-0), 100 Thieves (2-0), and KOI (2-0). Their form wavers – no clean stomps – but Worlds magic endures. Anyone’s Legend shock the field: 1-0 over Hanwha Life, Gen.G, and a 2-1 vs CTBC Flying Oyster. Split 1-1 in two prior international semis. Faker’s perfect playoff record vs Chinese teams (no losses) hangs in the balance. This grinds to five maps – no easy path for either. Both finished groups at 3-1, sole losses to Anyone’s Legend, setting up a domestic bloodbath where mid-lane duels and vision wars will decide scaling supremacy. evidences.title: T1 League of Legends Esports Team Stats, Roster, Matches & Results
evidences.content: This roster was assembled after the end of the 2024 season. Doran joined T1 as the new top laner, replacing Zeus. The team retained its core lineup of Oner, Faker, Gumayusi, and Keria, who have played together for several years and are considered one of the most coordinated squads in the LCK. When this updated roster was first formed, the team faced problems and showed inconsistent results early in the season, losing some important matches. However, as the split progressed, T1 managed to improve their form and secured second place in the LCK Spring 2025 Road to MSI, earning a spot on the international stage. [...] This lineup is considered T1’s first complete and iconic roster, debuting in OGN Champions Spring 2013 and setting the stage for their legendary run. While they fell short that split, they quickly refined their teamwork and strategy. In the following season, OGN Champions Summer 2013, this roster dominated the competition and went on to claim T1’s first World Championship title with a commanding 3:0 victory in the final. Now let’s look at the current T1 roster, considered one of the most talented in the world and built for the 2025 season: [...] They are now performing...

evidences.title: Everything YOU Need to Know About T1 for Worlds 2025
evidences.content: T1 probably should have lost. He was an especially great standout in their second series against DK where he was easily the best player in the match by a significant margin. Owner has looked great on every meta jungler not named Kiana. He's easily the best Jarvin in the world. He's had great games on Silus, Pantheon, Noctturn, Wukong, and basically anything else you can think of right now. I would say in playoffs at least, Owner has definitely been T1's best player. Although you could make an argument for Carrier at points as well. With owner in the form he's been showing, he's definitely ready to make a world's three repeat. It's just up to the rest of the team to keep up with him. Faker is still the mid laner for T1. And he also just signed another 4-year contract as well. There's a [...] an up and down season, but it was trending pretty well for the back half. They had a massive blow up for LCK split 2 playoffs, and they carried that momentum into MSI where they dismantled the LPL1 seed and took Geng to five games in grand finals. when they came back for split three. They also looked very strong in regular season and were a clear top two team in the LCK. However, their playof...

evidences.title: T1 defeats KT Rolster to win League of Legends Worlds 2025
evidences.content: In the third game, T1 suffered heavily at the hands of Cuzz’s Mundo. Lacking the damage to burst through KT’s tank, T1 could only watch as dragons, barons and eventually its own nexus fell to the enemy. At match point, KT had to go up against a Kalista first-pick and Faker’s Anivia. Moreover, thanks to critical Smites from Oner, T1 claimed every single neutral objective and took the series to game five. For the final game, T1 drafted a deadly Galio/Camille ult-combo. While a couple of oversteps from the LCK giant nearly allowed KT to make a comeback, T1 ultimately emerged victorious from the last crucial teamfights. As a result, T1 remains nearly undefeated in the Telecom War as the organisation lifts its sixth World Championship trophy. LoL Worlds 2025 MVP Gumayushi [...] But following its victories over Anyone’s Legend (3-2) and Top Esports (3-0), T1 and Faker were only one series away from their sixth Worlds title. A victory in today’s Grand Final would also make T1 the first team in history to win three consecutive LoL Championships. ## T1 Makes LoL History With Sixth Worlds Trophy LoL Worlds 2025 trophy Image credit: Colin Young-Wolff/Riot Games The Worlds showdown in Cheng...
